BELLESOGNO
✦  The Maison
"More than fragrance.
A signature identity."

Born in the ateliers of Paris, Bellesogno emerged from a singular obsession — to create scent as living art. Each fragrance is composed by master perfumers with decades of craft, sourced from the world's most precious raw materials.

We do not merely create perfume. We architect presence — the invisible signature you leave in every room, on every memory, in every encounter. Three fragrances. Three philosophies. One maison.

ORIGINS
✦  From the Earth

The Ingredients Journey

Every note travels thousands of miles before it reaches your skin.

🌿
Rajasthan, India
Sandalwood
Found in Noir Pulse

Indian Sandalwood is aged a minimum of 30 years before harvest. Its creamy, woody warmth is the most enduring base in perfumery — the final note that stays on skin for hours after everything else has faded.

Noir Pulse
🫚
Guatemala & Sri Lanka
Cardamom
Found in Amour

Guatemalan Cardamom is richer and more complex than its Asian counterparts. Hand-harvested at peak ripeness, it provides the opening warmth of Amour — the note that makes strangers turn their heads.

Amour
🍊
Sicily, Italy
Blood Mandarin
Found in Lumen

Sicilian Blood Mandarin is cold-pressed from the rind at sunrise, when essential oil yield is highest. It carries a brightness impossible to replicate synthetically — the quality that gives Lumen its luminosity.

Lumen
🍎
Normandy, France
Red Apple
Found in Noir Pulse

French Red Apple in perfumery is tart, almost metallic, with a crisp edge that cuts through heavier notes. In Noir Pulse, it creates the electric tension of the heart — seductive but never obvious.

Noir Pulse
🍦
Madagascar
Bourbon Vanilla
Found in Amour

Madagascar Bourbon Vanilla is hand-pollinated, sun-cured for six months. It is the base of Amour: the warmth that doesn't leave, the softness that remembers.

Amour
🌹
Grasse, France
Rose Centifolia
Found in Lumen

Harvested by hand before dawn in the rose capital of the world. It takes 500kg of petals to produce just 100g of absolute. This is what makes Lumen extraordinary.

Lumen
